articles.title: TESTING
articles.summary: If slow QA processes bottleneck you or your software engineering team and you're releasing slower because of it — you need to check out QA Wolf.

articles.title: Schedule a demo to learn more
CONSISTENT HASHING
articles.summary: Consistent hashing is an algorithm that minimizes key recomputation when a hash table's size changes, specifically in distributed caching scenarios. The traditional hashing approach suffers from complete cache invalidation upon node additions or removals. Consistent hashing maps both nodes and items onto a circle, assigning items to the nearest node clockwise, limiting the impact of node changes.

articles.title: WHICH TABLE FORMAT DO LLMS UNDERSTAND BEST? (RESULTS FOR 11 FORMATS)
articles.summary: This study tested 11 different data formats for LLM understanding by feeding GPT-4.1-nano 1,000 employee records and assessing its ability to answer questions accurately. Markdown-KV had the highest accuracy, while CSV and JSONL performed poorly, suggesting that format impacts LLM comprehension a lot. However, Markdown-KV also used more tokens than the most token-efficient format of CSV.

articles.title: LORA WITHOUT REGRET
articles.summary: The Thinking Machines Lab published research on Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA), a more efficient fine-tuning method for LLMs. Their findings show that LoRA can achieve similar performance to full fine-tuning when applied to all layers, especially MLP layers, and when not constrained by capacity limitations relative to dataset size. The study also found that optimal learning rates for LoRA are consistently 10x higher than those for full fine-tuning, and offers insights into setting LoRA hyperparameters.

articles.title: HOW I INFLUENCE TECH COMPANY POLITICS AS A STAFF SOFTWARE ENGINEER
articles.summary: While engineers are often tools in the game of corporate politics, they can strategically align their skills and ideas with company-wide mandates or executive priorities. By preparing multiple technical solutions that address various organizational concerns, engineers can proactively offer solutions when the "flavor of the month" arises. This allows engineers to control the direction of technical programs and avoid the implementation of less effective ideas.

articles.title: FOCUS IS SAYING NO. SOFTWARE MODERNIZATION PROJECTS DILEMMA
articles.summary: It's important to prioritize tasks that drive measurable business impact over technical improvements that lack user value. This dev spent time focusing on software modernization without considering business priorities, which ended up slowing down his career advancement.

articles.title: FIVE YEARS AS A STARTUP CTO: HOW, WHY, AND WAS IT WORTH IT?
articles.summary: This author, who has five years of experience as a startup CTO, goes over the challenges of building a tech team, transitioning to a new platform, and the personal sacrifices involved.

articles.title: THE SIMPLE WAY TO ADD DOMAIN SEARCH, PURCHASE, AND SETUP TO YOUR APP
articles.summary: Make onboarding and deployment easier for your customers with integrated domain features - without building them from scratch. The name.com API is simple, scalable, and built for AI-assisted development. It's free and quick to sign up, so you can get your API token and start building today.

articles.title: INTRODUCING THE JULES API
articles.summary: The Jules API allows for programmatic access to Jules' capabilities for automation and integration into software development workflows. The API allows for custom integrations with tools like Slack and project management software, as well as integration into CI/CD pipelines.

articles.title: SERA UI
articles.summary: Sera UI is an open-source UI component library built for React and Next.js, using Tailwind CSS for easy customization and responsive design. It has pre-built, reusable components with engaging animations powered by Framer Motion and built-in Lucide React icons.

articles.title: RUN
articles.summary: Run is a command-line tool built in Rust that acts as a polyglot command runner and REPL for over 25 programming languages. It simplifies multi-language development by providing a unified interface for executing code, removing the need to manage individual compilers and interpreters.

articles.title: SORA UPDATE #1
articles.summary: Sora is implementing two major changes based on user feedback: granting rightsholders greater control over character generation, including options to limit or prevent their use, and exploring revenue-sharing models for videos featuring their characters. This control addresses rightsholders' interest in "interactive fan fiction" while allowing them to manage character usage.

articles.title: WHAT GPT-OSS LEAKS ABOUT OPENAI'S TRAINING DATA
ERA OF AI SLOP CLEANUP HAS BEGUN
articles.summary: The increasing use of AI and vibe coding is leading to a surge of poorly written code, creating a need for skilled devs to clean up the mess.

articles.title: THE BEST WORST HACK THAT SAVED OUR BACON
articles.summary: This dev's team averted a potential calendar system crash by implementing a temporary, unconventional solution (using negative integers for primary keys) to buy time for a proper migration without disrupting customer integrations.

articles.title: MANAGING CONTEXT ON THE CLAUDE DEVELOPER PLATFORM
articles.summary: Anthropic's Claude Developer Platform now offers context editing and a memory tool, powered by Claude Sonnet 4.5, to let devs build more capable AI agents that can handle long-running tasks by managing context windows.

articles.title: NIRS FIRE DESTROYS GOVERNMENT'S CLOUD STORAGE SYSTEM, NO BACKUPS AVAILABLE
articles.summary: A fire at the National Information Resources Service (NIRS) of South Korea destroyed the government's cloud storage system, resulting in the permanent loss of work files for 750,000 civil servants due to the lack of external backups.
